name: Reset purchase-cancellation refs on subscription screen remount
description: In `apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/subscription.tsx`, two refs track whether the user tapped "Check later" to cancel a purchase-confirmation poll (`restoreCancelledRef` line 167, `topUpCancelledRef` line 168). Both refs are only reset inside their respective handlers *after* the `mutateAsync` call — but if `mutateAsync` throws or the native purchase sheet is cancelled (caught at the `isPurchaseCancelledError` guard), the handler returns early before reaching the reset. With no mount-reset, a prior "Check later" on one visit leaves the ref stuck at `true` permanently, causing the *next* successful purchase's or restore's confirmation alert to be silently suppressed on screen remount.
acceptanceCriteria:
**Root cause (file:line):** `subscription.tsx:167-168` — refs declared; `subscription.tsx:1178` — `restoreCancelledRef.current = true` set on "Check later"; `subscription.tsx:1242` — `topUpCancelledRef.current = true`. Reset at `subscription.tsx:247` (restore) and `:517` (top-up) are inside handlers that run *after* `mutateAsync`, unreachable when the user cancels the native sheet (caught at lines ~320 / ~499).

**Fix:** Add a mount-only `useEffect` in `SubscriptionContent` that resets both refs: `useEffect(() => { restoreCancelledRef.current = false; topUpCancelledRef.current = false; }, [])`. This ensures each screen mount starts with a clean state regardless of prior navigation.

**Secondary (out of scope here, already flagged):** `topUpInFlightRef` cleared inside `handleRestore` at line 271 is a suspected copy-paste from `handleTopUp`, already comment-flagged in the source — do not fix here.

**Regression test (red-green):** In `apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/subscription.test.tsx`, write a test that: (1) renders the screen, (2) mocks a poll that returns `'confirmed'` after 1 tick, (3) simulates the "Check later" button press (sets `restoreCancelledRef.current = true`), (4) unmounts and remounts the component, (5) triggers `handleRestore` again and asserts the success alert IS shown (not suppressed). Without the fix the test fails; with it, it passes.
riskImpact: Silently suppresses IAP confirmation UX on remount; user completes a purchase but sees no feedback, may try to purchase again causing double-charge attempt.
